# UC-22.24.7 · Selective Disclosure Attribute Set Minimization Monitoring

## Description

Supports qualified trust service auditing, wallet security monitoring, and PKI integrity evidence expected under eIDAS trust frameworks.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for eIDAS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes trust and sourcetypes eudi:wallet:presentation. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review. Indexes required: `index=trust`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=eudi:wallet:presentation`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=trust` `eudi:wallet:presentation` — trust_service, serial, not_after, ocsp_url, http_status, wallet_id.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=trust sourcetype="eudi:wallet:presentation" earliest=-7d
| eval eidas_tag="sel"
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval days_to_exp=round((exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| stats count as events, min(days_to_exp) as min_days, sum(eval(http_status>=400)) as http_err by trust_service, eidas_tag
| eval uc_id="22.24.7"
| where min_days < 30 OR http_err>0 OR eidas_tag="susp"
| sort min_days
| head 200
```

Understanding this SPL

**Selective Disclosure Attribute Set Minimization Monitoring (eIDAS 2.0)** — Supports qualified trust service auditing, wallet security monitoring, and PKI integrity evidence expected under eIDAS trust frameworks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=trust` `eudi:wallet:presentation` — trust_service, serial, not_after, ocsp_url, http_status, wallet_id. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: trust; **sourcetype**: eudi:wallet:presentation. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=trust, sourcetype="eudi:wallet:presentation", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **eidas_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **exp_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_exp** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by trust_service, eidas_tag** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where min_days < 30 OR http_err>0 OR eidas_tag="susp"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (min_days), Table (trust_service), Time chart (http_err)

## SPL

```spl
index=trust sourcetype="eudi:wallet:presentation" earliest=-7d
| eval eidas_tag="sel"
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval days_to_exp=round((exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| stats count as events, min(days_to_exp) as min_days, sum(eval(http_status>=400)) as http_err by trust_service, eidas_tag
| eval uc_id="22.24.7"
| where min_days < 30 OR http_err>0 OR eidas_tag="susp"
| sort min_days
| head 200
```
